title: System Design Interview Walk-Through
description: An interviewer drops you a vague system-design prompt. Drive the four-step framework: scope, high-level design, deep dive, wrap-up.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Let's design a URL shortener. Take it however you'd like.

templates.text: Before I jump in, can I ask a few clarifying questions about scope?
templates.hint_zh: 我動手前,可以先問幾個範圍上的問題嗎?

templates.text: How many writes and reads per day, and do shortened links need to expire?
templates.hint_zh: 每天的寫入跟讀取量是多少?短連結有沒有過期需求?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: 100 million writes a day, 100 to one read-write ratio, and links live for ten years.

templates.text: Got it. Let me sketch the high-level pieces and do a quick back-of-the-envelope estimate.
templates.hint_zh: 了解。我先畫高層架構並做粗略估算。

templates.text: Three core pieces: an API service, a hash function for the short codes, and a key-value store for the mapping.
templates.hint_zh: 三個核心:API 服務、產生短碼的雜湊函式、儲存對應關係的 KV store。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Where would you go deep first?

templates.text: The hash function. Length, collision handling, and whether we encode counters or hash the URL directly.
templates.hint_zh: 雜湊函式。長度、碰撞處理,以及要編碼計數器還是直接雜湊 URL。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good. Let's say we're at the wrap-up. Anything you'd improve?

templates.text: Add a CDN cache layer for hot links and shard the database when we cross ten times the load.
templates.hint_zh: 熱門連結加 CDN 快取,流量過十倍時資料庫分片。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Nice structure. Thanks for walking me through it.

templates.text: Thank you. I appreciate the chance to think this through.
templates.hint_zh: 謝謝你,很感謝有機會把流程走過一遍。
